Wade buys three sandwiches and two drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * 2 = [total_drink_cost]. If Wade spends a total of [total_spent], then the amount spent on sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]. Since he buys three sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is ([total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]) / 3 = [cost_per_sandwich]. The lab [lab] is located at [address], and Wade plans to stop there on his way back.

Dividing this by the number of sandwiches, which is 3, gives the cost per sandwich, [cost_per_sandwich]. The lab [lab] is located at [address], and Wade plans to stop there on his way back.
